cover:
A high-quality architectural system block diagram illustrating the "AGENTFORCE CUSTOM ACTIONS".
Overall Theme: Technical architecture map, clean flow chart, analytical, educational, modern and well-structured layout.
Background: A very clean, subtle light gray background with faint grid lines.

Header:
- At the top-center, a massive dark bold rounded header block displaying "AGENTFORCE CUSTOM ACTIONS".
- Below the title, a small subtitle: "The Builder Playbook: Apex vs Flow vs Prompt Template vs API Action".
- In the top-right corner, a small circular profile icon labeled "Bennie Joseph | Architect".

Architecture Layers (Arranged hierarchically with clear connections):
- A "Action Types" container (colored in neon blue) containing modules: "Apex Invocable Action", "Flow Action", "Prompt Template Action", "API / External Action".
- A "Atlas Reasoning Engine v2" container (colored in neon green) containing modules: "Topic Classification", "Action Selection", "Confidence Score", "Multi-Agent Routing".
- A "Execution + Audit" container (colored in grey) containing modules: "Apex Commit Layer", "Idempotency Check", "Human Escalation", "Action Log".
- A central block at the heart of the system labeled "Agentforce 2.0 Orchestrator" with a rotating arrow icon. This central node has multiple dotted directional connector lines feeding into and out of all surrounding layers.
- An "INTEGRATION LAYER" box on the upper right with modules "Salesforce API v64.0" and "Data Cloud Vector Search".
- A bottom panel labeled "ACTION DESIGN RULES" with sub-modules "Single Responsibility", "Typed Contracts", "Confidence Required", "Testable at 200 records".

Style Details: Technical blueprint style, clean rounded rectangle containers, precise dotted lines with arrowheads showing control and data flow, soft pastel color scheme (pale yellows, soft blues, light greens, and dark blues), micro vector icons inside each block, neat modern sans-serif fonts, absolutely crisp rendering, no blurred elements, zero spelling mistakes, publication-ready engineering diagram.
images:
A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "ACTION SCHEMA DESIGN: God Action vs Single-Purpose Action — What Atlas Reasoning Engine Needs".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "ACTION SCHEMA DESIGN" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "God Action vs Single-Purpose Action — What Atlas Reasoning Engine Needs".

Left Column (❌ GOD ACTION — Atlas Cannot Reason):
- Header: "❌ GOD ACTION — Atlas Cannot Reason" in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "handleCase(caseId) — does classify + check + write + notify" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "No typed output — returns opaque string" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Atlas cannot retry individual failed steps" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "One failure → entire action rolls back" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Impossible to test — side effects everywhere" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Result: Atlas picks the action but cannot trust the output" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (✓ SINGLE-PURPOSE — Atlas Composes Safely):
- Header: "✓ SINGLE-PURPOSE — Atlas Composes Safely" in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "classifyCase() → { category, confidence, reason }" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "checkEntitlement() → { entitled, source, contractId }" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "draftResponse() → { subject, body, approvalRequired }" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "proposeCaseUpdate() → idempotency-safe proposal" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Atlas chains actions across topics with full confidence tracking" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Result: each action is retryable, auditable, and swappable" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.

A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "FLOW vs APEX FOR CUSTOM ACTIONS: 8 Factors — When to Use Each for Agentforce 2.0 Custom Actions".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "FLOW vs APEX FOR CUSTOM ACTIONS" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "8 Factors — When to Use Each for Agentforce 2.0 Custom Actions".

Left Column (USE FLOW WHEN...):
- Header: "USE FLOW WHEN..." in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Logic is declarative and admins need to maintain it" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Action needs screen elements or guided input" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Simple field updates, notifications, approval routing" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Business user wants to adjust the action without code" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Agentforce action is admin-configured workflow step" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Moderate data volume — no bulk DML or nested loops" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (USE APEX WHEN...):
- Header: "USE APEX WHEN..." in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Action processes 50+ records or has nested logic" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "You need deterministic behavior with full unit tests" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Action is shared across Agentforce + LWC + Queueable" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Confidence scoring, idempotency, or rollback needed" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Complex SOQL with dynamic filters or multiple objects" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Compliance requires every write to be fully audited" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.
